bids.reasoning: I am targeting maximum utility at the opening since there is no time pressure yet. The opponent’s first offer gives me low utility overall, with concessions only on issueD and issueE, so I will keep issueD as valueB since it is equally best for me while holding firm on the other high-value issues.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I targeted a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession to avoid simply repeating my previous offer. The opponent has improved their offer to me by moving issueA to my top value, but they are still holding issueB, issueC, and issueF at low values for me, so I will hold firm on the more important issues and concede slightly on low-weight issueF.

I am targeting a high early-stage utility around 0.924, since time is still very early and there is no need to concede deeply yet. The opponent has improved from very low utility offers to one that gives me valueA on issueA and valueB on issueC, so I will recognize that movement by conceding issueC to valueB while holding firm on high-importance issueB and issueF.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.964 because it is still very early (t=0.06), so there is little reason to concede heavily yet. The opponent has repeatedly pushed issueB=valueC and issueF=valueC, but those are costly or unattractive to me; I will make a small concession on the lower-weight issueF while holding firm on the more important issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early at t=0.08, so there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent’s latest valid offer improved substantially for me on issueA and issueC while still pushing issueB and issueF to valueC, so I will hold firm on the high-weight issueB but concede the lower-weight issueF to match their apparent preference. This also avoids repeating my exact previous bid.

I am targeting a high utility bid (about 0.964) because it is still very early at t=0.10, so there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent has consistently held issueB at valueC and issueF at valueC, but has shown flexibility on issueA and issueC while already giving favorable issueD and issueE values; I will hold firm on the important issueB and offer a moderate concession on the lower-weight issueF.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a limited concession on a lower-weight issue. The opponent has repeatedly signaled insistence on issueF: valueC and issueB: valueC, but issueB is high-importance for me, so I concede issueF while holding issueB at my best value.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.14, so there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent repeatedly offers issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, which are also good for me, and has shown some willingness on issueA=valueA and issueC=valueB/valueA, but they continue to push issueB=valueC and issueF=valueC, so I am holding firm on the more important issueB while varying the package from my last bid.

I am targeting a high but not maximal utility bid around 0.89, since it is still early at t=0.16 and there is no need for a large concession yet. The opponent has repeatedly signaled interest in issueD=valueB, issueE=valueA, issueF=valueC, and often accepts my preferred issueA/valueA and issueC/valueA, but they keep pushing issueB=valueC, which is costly for me, so I concede issueB only to valueA rather than valueC.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.18, so there is no need to concede on high-weight issues like issueB and issueC yet. The opponent has consistently demanded issueB=valueC and issueF=valueC, while often allowing my preferred issueA, issueD, and issueE values; I will concede issueF since it is lower weight for me, but hold firm on issueB and issueC for now.

I am targeting a high but slightly concessionary utility around 0.894 because it is still early at t=0.20, so I should not give up major weighted issues yet. The opponent consistently offers issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, which are already excellent for me, and they seem firm on issueF=valueC and issueB=valueC; I concede issueF because it is low-weight, while only softening issueB to valueA rather than the much worse valueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.82, conceding on lower-cost or opponent-signaled points while preserving value on issueA, issueD, and issueE. At t=0.22 there is still no need to accept their repeated low issueB=valueC demand, but their offers consistently signal issueF=valueC and often issueC=valueB, so I am using those concessions to improve acceptability without dropping too far.

I am targeting a high but slightly concessive utility around 0.894, since it is still early-mid negotiation at t=0.24 and there is no need to drop to the opponent’s repeated low offers. The opponent consistently insists on issueB=valueC and issueF=valueC while giving me strong values on issueA, issueD, and issueE, so I concede on the lower-weight issueF and partially on issueB, while holding issueC at my best value this round.

At t=0.26 there is still time, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 rather than conceding heavily. I am targeting about 0.89 utility, since it is still early-mid negotiation and there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent has been very firm on issueB=valueC and issueF=valueC while usually accepting issueA=valueA, issueD=valueB, and issueE=valueA; I concede the low-cost issueF and make a small concession on issueB while holding the high-value issueC at valueA.

I am targeting a utility around 0.82: I concede on issueC and issueF, which the opponent has repeatedly pushed toward valueB/valueC, while avoiding the very costly concession of issueB to valueC. At t=0.32 I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.84, since there is time left and no need to concede heavily on high-weight issues yet. The opponent has consistently signaled interest in issueD=valueB, issueE=valueA, and issueF=valueC, so I concede issueF while holding stronger positions on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a strong but more conciliatory utility around 0.82, since we are still before the midpoint but the opponent has been very consistent on issueB=valueC, issueD=valueB, issueE=valueA, and issueF=valueC. Their offers to me are fairly stable rather than steadily improving, so I will concede on lower-cost/less critical points like issueC and issueF while holding issueB at valueA instead of the costly valueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 while still preserving concessions on lower-weight/previously contested areas, especially accepting issueF: valueC. Time is still mid-negotiation, so I do not need to fully accept the opponent’s latest 0.844-utility offer yet; their latest move to issueB: valueB shows some concession, and they have previously offered issueC: valueA, so I will test that combination.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility offer around 0.888, conceding issueC to valueB while keeping the important gains on issueA, issueB, issueD, and issueE. At t=0.40 there is moderate time pressure but not enough to simply mirror the opponent’s latest offer; their recent move to issueB:valueB suggests some concession, while they remain firm on issueF:valueC, so I am testing a modest improvement there.

I am targeting a high utility of about 0.92 by keeping my strongest positions on issueA, issueB, issueC, issueD, and issueE while conceding issueF to valueC, which the opponent has consistently demanded. At t=0.42 there is moderate time pressure, and the opponent’s latest offer has improved substantially for me, especially by moving issueB to valueB and issueC to valueA, so matching it is a good way to lock in a strong agreement.
